unit: Unit 12: Everyday Life with Your Computer
title: Printing from Your Computer
drDigitalIntro:
Printing lets you put what's on your screen onto paper. Try it right now!

1. Open your web browser and find any page you'd like to print — a recipe, an article, or just this lesson.
2. Press Ctrl+P (or Cmd+P on a Mac).
3. The Print dialog appears, showing a preview of what will print.
4. Choose your printer from the dropdown (if one is connected).
5. Click Print.

If you don't have a printer:
• The Print dialog still opens — you can choose "Save as PDF" instead of a real printer.
• This creates a file you can email or take to a library or office supply store to print there.

Scanning works the other way: a scanner turns paper into a digital file. Many printers include a scanner on top — lift the lid, place your document face-down, and use the scanner software. Phone cameras with document scanning (built into most camera apps) work in a pinch too.
drDigitalSuccess: Ctrl+P (or Cmd+P) works in almost every app — browser, email, documents, everything. And Save as PDF is your backup when there's no printer around.
drDigitalHint: Press Ctrl+P (or Cmd+P on Mac) in your browser to see the print dialog.
